IMPORTANT PROCESS NOTE — read this first:
In R8 you wrote PASS verdicts that masked real bugs the user saw on playtest. From now on the verdict process is enforced like this: for every fix attempt you must (1) capture screenshots into a fresh per-iteration subdir, (2) write structured observations BEFORE writing a verdict — actual visual descriptions of what you see, not boilerplate, (3) if observations diverge from expected, fix the code, re-capture into the next iteration's subdir, re-observe, and only declare PASS once observations match. Every iteration is a separate commit on the round branch so the loop is auditable. Receipt must include an iteration_log array. status=blocked if any final-iteration observation still does not match expected.

BUG A — CHAT X CLOSE GLYPH RENDERED BEHIND THE CHAT BAND'S MESSAGE AREA.

  Repro: open the watch card expanded with the chat band visible. The user reports the X close button (added in R8 D1) appears BEHIND the brown 'No messages yet' message-region chrome rather than ON TOP. The hit region is correct (clicking still works) but the glyph is occluded.

  Root cause is render order in game/ui/chat_panel.py render_watch_band: the X is being drawn before the message-region chrome (or its background fill), so the chrome paints over it.

  Required fix:
    1. In render_watch_band, draw the X close glyph LAST (after divider, message region chrome, input row).
    2. Behind the X glyph, draw a small dark/black rounded square (e.g., 14x14 with 2px border-radius, RGB ~(20,20,28), 1px border ~(60,60,80)) so the X looks like an actual button. The glyph itself stays the same visible size.
    3. The X background rect should sit flush with the band's top-right inner edge (e.g., 2-3px inset from the band's top and right). Update _watch_band_close_rect to match the new rect bounds for hit testing.

  Acceptance: pixel sample inside the X background rect's center after a render — assert the sampled color is close to the X glyph color, NOT the brown message-region color. Add a regression test in tests/test_wk52_watch_card.py: test_chat_close_x_renders_on_top_of_band.

BUG B — BUILDING CARD IS STILL TOO WIDE AND SHOWS A 'BUILDINGTYPE...' HYBRID INSTEAD OF A CLEAN BUILDING CARD.

  Repro from the user's playtest: clicking on a building in-game (not the screenshot-scenario shortcut) shows a wider-than-LEFT_COL_W card with the header text reading 'Buildingtype...' (truncated placeholder) and a hybrid layout that looks like the watch card with building stats jammed in. The user's read: the screenshot scenario validated one path, but the real click flow goes through different code.

  Investigate AND fix all of these:

    (a) Width mismatch: the building card likely renders at a width inherited from a stale rect (perhaps the right-panel rect that R4 zeroed) instead of LEFT_COL_W=224. Find the actual render path for selected_building in HUD.render() and confirm the rect width passed to InfoCard.draw_shell is exactly LEFT_COL_W.

    (b) Header text 'Buildingtype...': the display name lookup is wrong. Building entities expose a building_type attribute (string like 'castle', 'inn') and a get_display_name() or human-readable name elsewhere. The header should show the display name, title-cased (e.g., 'Castle', 'Inn'). Find where 'Buildingtype' string is being constructed (probably .__class__.__name__ or similar) and replace with the proper display path.

    (c) Hybrid rendering: the user describes a 'sort of weird building hero card kind of thing' — meaning hero card sections (hero map view, stats grid, chat band) bleed into the building card render. The card-slot dispatcher must FULLY swap, not paint hero sections then overwrite some with building data. Audit _render_card_slot and the section choices for the building branch.

    (d) Real-click path != screenshot-scenario path: the wk52_building_card screenshot you added in R8 sets eng.selected_building directly. In actual gameplay the click flows through input_handler -> engine select handler -> sets selected_building AND clears other state. Make the screenshot scenario use the SAME entry point as a real click (call the engine's click handler with synthetic mouse coords, OR call the same select method the click handler invokes). The shipped path and the verified path must be identical.

  Required fixes (apply all that match the actual cause):
    1. Building card rect: explicit width=LEFT_COL_W, x=0; same edge-anchored geometry as the hero card.
    2. Building card header: use a clear display-name path. Add a tiny helper if needed: `building_display_name(building) -> str` that returns building.get_display_name() if it exists else building.building_type.replace('_',' ').title() else building.__class__.__name__.replace('Building','').strip() or 'Building'. Document the chosen path in the receipt.
    3. Card-slot dispatcher: confirm the hero/building branches do not share rendering — only the InfoCard chrome (header + chevron + body bg) is shared via draw_shell. Body painters are mutually exclusive.
    4. Screenshot scenario for building card: rewrite the apply callback to mimic the real click path — find the engine's selection method (e.g., engine.select_building(b) or engine.handle_world_click(building.center_x, building.center_y)) and invoke it. Document which method you called.

  Acceptance:
    - Building card width measured from the rendered PNG: 224 ± 1px.
    - Header text reads the building's display name (e.g., 'Castle'), NOT 'Buildingtype' or 'Buildingtype...' or anything similar.
    - No hero map sub-rect, no stats grid, no chat band visible inside the building card.
    - Heroes column above the card visible and unobstructed.
    - Real-click code path and screenshot-scenario path produce identical card rendering.

MANDATORY ITERATE-LOOP PROCESS:
  Run the loop separately for Bug A and Bug B (they can share iteration numbers if you prefer, or use suffixes — your call). For each iteration:
    1. mkdir docs/screenshots/wk52_phase3/iter_N/
    2. Run capture: python3 tools/capture_screenshots.py --scenario wk52_pin_alerts --seed 3 --out docs/screenshots/wk52_phase3/iter_N --size 1920x1080 --ticks 480
    3. Open the relevant PNG(s) (read_file or your image-view tool).

Example for Bug A: 'I see: chat band visible, divider present, brown message-region fills lower 80%, top-right corner shows partial X glyph but the brown chrome overlaps roughly half of it. Expected: X fully on top with dark button background. Diff: order of draws still wrong.' If observations match expected, mark verdict PASS for that shot. If not, write what you'll change in the code (one or two lines), apply the change, commit, then start iteration N+1.
    5. Each iteration commits its code changes separately. Use git commit messages like 'R9 iter N: Bug A render-order fix attempt' so the history is readable.
    6. Continue until ALL relevant shots are PASS.

  Maximum 5 iterations per bug. If still FAIL after 5: status=blocked, list the remaining FAIL observations in blockers, do NOT push the SDK completion-success receipt. PM (Agent 01) will iterate.

CONTEXT: Jaimie playtested after R10 and found three issues. Screenshot at:

Read that screenshot before starting. It shows the Warrior Guild building menu overlapping the hero card and other panels, shifted too far right, and scroll wheel not working inside menus.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
BUG A — BUILDING MENU OVERLAPS HERO CARD / RIGHT PANELS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

The building menu panel (showing Warrior Guild info, hero list, Demolish/Enter Building buttons) renders ON TOP of the hero card and other right-side panels. It should be in the same left-column space, same z-layer, aligned inline — NOT hovering over other elements.

Root cause investigation:
  - The building panel likely uses its own absolute positioning (building_panel.py hardcodes panel_x, panel_y) instead of using the HUD's layout rects from _layout_rects_for_screen().
  - The hero panel uses the `left` rect from HUD layout (starts at x=0, y=top_h, width=left_w, height=left_h). The building panel should use the SAME rect.
  - Check whether building_panel.render() is called with a different rect or uses internal absolute positioning that doesn't match the hero panel's rect.

Required changes:
  1. Make the building panel use the SAME left-column rect that the hero panel uses. Both panels should receive their position from _layout_rects_for_screen() `left` rect.
  2. The building panel must NOT render outside or overlapping the left-column area. No covering the hero card, watch card, or bottom bar.
  3. If building_panel.py has its own panel_x/panel_y hardcoded values, replace them with the rect passed from HUD.
  4. The building panel and hero panel are mutually exclusive (only one shows at a time in the left column) — so they should share the same space, not stack.

Acceptance:
  - Open building menu while hero card is pinned — building menu is contained within the left column, does NOT overlap the hero card or any bottom-left elements.
  - Building menu left/top edge matches exactly where the hero menu would appear.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
BUG B — BUILDING MENU X-OFFSET 10-20px TOO FAR RIGHT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

The building menu's left edge is shifted ~10-20px to the right compared to the hero menu. They should have identical x-positions.

Root cause: building_panel.py likely hardcodes `panel_x = 10` (or similar margin) while the hero panel renders at x=0 (the left edge of the `left` rect). This creates a visible offset.

Required changes:
  1. Remove the hardcoded x-offset in building_panel.py. The panel_x should come from the layout rect, same as the hero panel.
  2. Both panels must start at the same x-coordinate.

Acceptance:
  - Building menu left edge is pixel-identical to hero menu left edge.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
BUG C — SCROLL WHEEL INSIDE MENUS STILL ZOOMS MAP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

When the mouse cursor is inside an open menu panel (hero menu or building menu), using the scroll wheel should scroll the panel content — but it still zooms the game map instead.

R10 added scroll support in input_handler.py, but it is not working in the live game (Ursina mode). The issue is likely one of:
  1. Ursina's own input system intercepts scroll events before they reach InputHandler/pygame events.
  2. The handle_menu_scroll() check in input_handler.py returns False (does not consume the event) so zoom proceeds.
  3. The rect check inside handle_menu_scroll() doesn't match the actual visible panel rect because of the building panel positioning bug.

Required investigation + fix:
  1. Trace the scroll event path in Ursina mode. Check if ursina.input() or the Ursina camera controller eats scroll events. If so, intercept scroll in the HUD layer BEFORE Ursina processes it.
  2. In game/input_handler.py, verify handle_menu_scroll() correctly checks whether the mouse is over an ACTIVE menu panel. The rect must match the actual rendered panel rect (not a stale/wrong rect from before Bug A/B are fixed).
  3. When handle_menu_scroll() returns True (scroll consumed by a menu), the event MUST be suppressed — no zoom should happen.
  4. If Ursina has its own scroll→zoom binding, you may need to gate it: check `hud.is_mouse_over_menu(pos)` before allowing zoom.
  5. Check game/engine_facades/ or game/engine.py for the camera zoom handler and add the menu-hover guard there.

Acceptance:
  - Hover over open menu, scroll wheel → menu scrolls, map does NOT zoom.
  - Move cursor off menu, scroll wheel → map zooms normally.
  - Must work in Ursina mode (python main.py) — this is the mode Jaimie uses.

pm_agent_prompts.08:
You are activated for sprint wk52_attachment_phase3_radar_alerts_memorial, round wk52_r12_menu_scroll_wheel (single-bug round — SCROLL WHEEL ROUTING).

## THE PROBLEM

When the mouse cursor is hovering over an open menu panel (hero menu or building menu in the left column), the scroll wheel STILL zooms the game map instead of scrolling the menu content. This has been attempted in R10 and R11 and NEITHER fix works in Ursina mode.

The player launches with `python main.py --provider mock` (Ursina renderer). Your fix MUST work in this mode.

## WHY PRIOR FIXES FAILED

R10/R11 added `handle_menu_scroll()` to `game/input_handler.py` and tried to consume pygame scroll events. But **Ursina has its own input system** that processes scroll events independently. The Ursina camera/zoom controller reads scroll input through Ursina's `input()` function or `mouse.scroll` attribute — it does NOT go through the pygame event queue. So even though `handle_menu_scroll()` might consume the pygame event, Ursina's zoom handler never sees that consumption and zooms anyway.

## YOUR INVESTIGATION PLAN (do this BEFORE writing any code)

1. **Find where Ursina zoom happens.** Search for scroll/zoom handling in:
   - `game/engine.py` — look for scroll, zoom, mouse_scroll, input() method
   - `game/engine_facades/` — camera/display facades
   - `game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py` — Ursina-specific input
   - Any Ursina `input()` function override or `Entity.input()` method
   - Search for: `mouse.scroll`, `scroll_y`, `held_keys`, `zoom`, `camera.fov`, `camera.y` or similar

2. **Find where menu panels track their screen rects.** The HUD renders hero_panel and building_panel into the `left` rect from `_layout_rects_for_screen()`. You need to know the exact pixel rect of whatever panel is currently visible so you can do a point-in-rect hover check.

3. **Find where HUD exposes hover state.** Check if `hud.py` has an `is_mouse_over_menu()` or `is_mouse_over_panel()` method. If not, you'll create one.

## THE FIX PATTERN

The fix must intercept scroll at the Ursina zoom level, NOT the pygame event level. The pattern is:

```
# In whatever function/method handles Ursina zoom:
def handle_scroll_zoom(scroll_amount):
    # CHECK: is mouse over any open menu panel?
    if hud.is_mouse_over_menu(mouse_position):
        # Route scroll to the menu instead
        hud.scroll_active_menu(scroll_amount)
        return  # DO NOT ZOOM
    # Normal zoom behavior
    apply_zoom(scroll_amount)
```

Concretely:

1. **Add `is_mouse_over_menu(pos) -> bool` to HUD** (if not already present). This checks whether `pos` is inside the rect of any currently-visible, scrollable menu panel (hero_panel, building_panel). Use the actual rendered rect, not a hardcoded one.

2. **Add `scroll_active_menu(direction: int)` to HUD.** This adjusts the scroll_offset of whichever menu panel is currently visible. `direction` is +1 (scroll down) or -1 (scroll up). Scroll by ~20-30px per tick. Clamp to [0, max_scroll] where max_scroll = max(0, content_height - visible_height).

3. **Gate the zoom handler.** In the Ursina zoom code path (wherever you found it in step 1), add the `is_mouse_over_menu()` check BEFORE applying zoom. If the mouse is over a menu, call `scroll_active_menu()` instead and return early.

4. **Handle mouse position correctly.** In Ursina mode, the mouse position may be in Ursina screen coordinates, not pygame coordinates. Make sure you convert correctly. `pygame.mouse.get_pos()` returns pygame window coords. Ursina's `mouse.position` returns normalized [-0.5, 0.5] coords. Use whichever coordinate system matches your panel rects.

## WHAT FILES YOU MAY EDIT

- `game/ui/hud.py` — add is_mouse_over_menu(), scroll_active_menu()
- `game/ui/building_panel.py` — add/fix scroll_offset support
- `game/ui/hero_panel.py` — add/fix scroll_offset support
- `game/engine.py` — gate the zoom handler
- `game/engine_facades/render_coordinator.py` or camera/display facade — gate zoom if that's where it lives
- `game/input_handler.py` — update if needed
- `game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py` — only if zoom lives here
- `tests/test_wk52_r10_menu_scroll.py` — update/add tests

## VERIFICATION

You CANNOT verify scroll behavior from a static screenshot. Instead:

1. **Write a test** that:
   - Creates a mock HUD with a visible panel rect
   - Calls `is_mouse_over_menu(point_inside)` and asserts True
   - Calls `is_mouse_over_menu(point_outside)` and asserts False
   - Calls `scroll_active_menu(1)` and asserts scroll_offset increased
   - Calls `scroll_active_menu(-1)` enough times and asserts scroll_offset is clamped to 0

2. **Write a test** that verifies the zoom handler checks `is_mouse_over_menu()` before zooming. This can be a unit test that mocks the HUD and verifies the zoom function returns early when the method returns True.

3. Run all gates:
   ```
   python3 -m pytest tests/ -v
   python3 tools/qa_smoke.py --quick
   ```

4. In your agent log, describe EXACTLY which function/method you found handles Ursina zoom, what file and line number, and how you gated it. This is critical for PM review.

CONTEXT: Jaimie playtested after R12 (scroll wheel works). Four items to fix.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ITEM A — CHAT CLOSED BY DEFAULT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

When a hero is pinned or selected and the watch card appears, the chat band should start COLLAPSED (closed). The player can open it manually by clicking the chat expand button.

Required changes:
  1. Find where the chat band expanded state is initialized or set on pin/select. Set it to collapsed by default.
  2. The chat band should only open when the user explicitly clicks to expand it.

Acceptance: Pin a hero — chat band is collapsed. Card is more compact.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ITEM B — HERO CARD ACTIONS MUST NOT CLOSE OTHER MENUS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

Currently, closing chat on the hero card, or minimizing/closing the hero card itself, also closes whatever building menu or hero menu is open in the left column. These should be completely independent.

The hero card (bottom-left watch card) and the left-column menus (building panel, hero panel) are separate UI elements. Actions on one should NEVER affect the other.

Required investigation:
  1. Find where the hero card close/minimize action handler fires. It likely calls something that clears `selected_building` or `selected_hero` or triggers a deselection.
  2. The card X button / minimize should ONLY affect the card state (`_watch_card_expanded`, `_chat_expanded`, etc.). It must NOT call any method that deselects buildings, heroes, or closes left-column panels.
  3. Check `handle_click()` in hud.py — the click handler may be returning an action like `deselect` or `None` that the engine interprets as "close everything".

Acceptance:
  - Open building menu + hero card. Close chat on card. Building menu stays.
  - Open hero menu + hero card. Minimize card. Hero menu stays.
  - Close card entirely. Left-column menu stays.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ITEM C — REMOVE BLANK SPACE WHEN CHAT IS CLOSED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

When the chat band is collapsed, there is a ~25-50px blank area below the last content row (XP/Level/Mana) before the card's bottom edge. The card height should be DYNAMIC — shrink when chat is closed so it's snug.

Required changes:
  1. The card height constants (WATCH_CARD_FULL_H, WATCH_CARD_STATS_H, etc.) should adjust based on whether chat is expanded or collapsed.
  2. When chat is closed: card_height = WATCH_CARD_HEADER_H + WATCH_CARD_MAP_H + WATCH_CARD_STATS_H (no chat band height).
  3. When chat is open: card_height = above + WATCH_CARD_CHAT_H.
  4. The blank padding at the bottom should be eliminated — no empty space between last content row and card border.

Acceptance: With chat closed, the card bottom edge is flush against the last content. No visible gap.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ITEM D — ENTER BUILDING CENTERED MODAL OVERLAY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

The 'Enter Building' button on the building menu currently does nothing (the old right-panel interior view was removed). Replace it with a centered modal overlay.

Design (follow the Memorial Card pattern):
  1. Dark semi-transparent fullscreen background (same as MemorialCard.OVERLAY_ALPHA).
  2. Centered card: ~480px wide, ~400px tall (adjust to content).
  3. Header: building name + type (e.g. 'Warrior Guild — Interior').
  4. Body: list of heroes currently inside the building. For each hero show: name, class, level, HP. If no heroes inside, show 'No heroes inside.' placeholder text.
  5. Footer: 'Close' button that dismisses the overlay and unpauses.
  6. Opening the overlay pauses the game. Closing unpauses.

Implementation approach:
  1. Create a new class `BuildingInteriorOverlay` in `game/ui/building_interior_overlay.py` (or add to an existing overlay file). Follow the MemorialCard pattern closely.
  2. The overlay has `show(building)`, `hide()`, `render(surface)`, `handle_click(pos)` methods.
  3. Wire it into HUD: `self.building_interior_overlay = BuildingInteriorOverlay()`
  4. In the building menu click handler, when 'Enter Building' is clicked: `self.building_interior_overlay.show(selected_building)` and return an action that pauses the game.
  5. In HUD.render(), if the overlay is visible, render it on top of everything (same z-order as memorial card).
  6. In the click handler, check `building_interior_overlay.handle_click(pos)` — if Close is clicked, hide and return unpause action.
  7. In engine.py handle_hud_action(), add handlers for the open/close overlay actions (pause/unpause).

Data source for heroes inside:
  - Buildings have a `heroes_inside` list or similar attribute. Check `game/entities/buildings/base.py` for the attribute name.
  - If the building has no such attribute, check `occupants`, `guests`, or search for how the old interior view got its data.

Acceptance:
  - Click Enter Building → centered overlay appears, game pauses.
  - Overlay shows building name and heroes inside (or 'No heroes' placeholder).
  - Click Close → overlay dismissed, game unpauses.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ITERATE-LOOP (visual items A, C, D)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════

sprint_meta.notes: Performance optimization sprint. Game running at 10-20 FPS with hero spring-forward artifacts. Three root causes identified: (1) per-frame PIL texture pipeline, (2) pathfinding storms without global frame budget, (3) frame-rate dependent movement with no render interpolation. Target: stable 40-60 FPS with smooth hero movement.

round_meta.build_target: Replace per-frame PIL texture conversion with pre-baked atlas lookups — target +20-30 FPS gain

pm_status_summary.summary: R1: Fix the #1 bottleneck — per-frame pygame→PIL→GPU texture conversion for ~66 unit sprites. Replace with pre-baked texture atlas (UV lookup per frame, no PIL per tick). Agent 10 profiles baseline + designs atlas strategy, Agent 03 implements renderer changes, Agent 11 validates FPS gain + visual correctness.

pm_context.problem: Every frame, for every visible unit (~66 entities), the renderer calls _unit_anim_surface() then TerrainTextureBridge.surface_to_texture() which runs pygame.image.tobytes() + PIL.Image.frombytes() + synchronous GPU upload. Cost: 8-12ms/frame (50-70% of frame budget). All single-threaded, blocking.
affected_files: game/graphics/unit_atlas.py (existing atlas builder — currently only used by instanced path)
pm_context.existing_atlas: unit_atlas.py already has a UnitAtlasBuilder that packs all frames into a 2048x2048 atlas. Currently only used when KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCING=1 (disabled by default due to visual bugs). The atlas concept is proven but not wired into the legacy billboard path.
pm_context.target_architecture: Pre-render ALL animation frames into the atlas at startup. Per-frame rendering becomes: lookup (unit_type, anim_clip, frame_index) -> UV rect in atlas -> update billboard UV coords. No PIL, no pygame.image.tobytes(), no per-frame GPU upload. Single atlas texture stays resident on GPU.
secondary_fixes_this_round: Cache clips_for() at unit spawn instead of per-frame (save 2-4ms/frame)
Skip ent.position = pos_xyz when position unchanged (avoid Panda3D transform invalidation)
Replace time.time() with frame-indexed timing in _unit_anim_state (fix Windows 15ms jitter)

10.next_action: Profile baseline frame times (tick_simulation, ursina_renderer, hud_texture_upload). Design atlas integration strategy for legacy billboard path. Write perf baseline + target metrics.

03.next_action: Implement texture atlas integration into legacy billboard renderer. Replace _unit_anim_surface() + surface_to_texture() hot path with atlas UV lookups. Wire unit_atlas.py into the default (non-instanced) render path.

11.next_action: Run perf_ursina_stress.py before and after. Verify FPS improvement (target: >35 FPS with 30 heroes + 60 enemies). Visual smoke test: all unit types animate correctly, no missing sprites.

pm_send_list_minimal.rationale: Agent 10 profiles first to establish baseline metrics, then Agent 03 implements the atlas pipeline change (high complexity — renderer architecture), then Agent 11 validates FPS + visual correctness.

pm_agent_prompts.03:
You are activated for sprint wk60_v16_perf_optimization, round wk60_r1_texture_atlas_pipeline.

READ FIRST:
- .cursor/plans/v1.6_performance_analysis.md (perf analysis)
- .cursor/plans/wk60_r1_perf_baseline_and_strategy.md (Agent 10's profiling + strategy — written in wave 1)
- game/graphics/unit_atlas.py (existing atlas builder)
- game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py (the hot path you're optimizing)
- game/graphics/terrain_texture_bridge.py (the expensive PIL bridge to replace)
- game/graphics/hero_sprite_library.py (clips_for)

YOUR ASSIGNMENT — IMPLEMENT TEXTURE ATLAS PIPELINE:

The current per-frame path costs 8-12ms:
  _unit_anim_surface() -> pygame Surface -> TerrainTextureBridge.surface_to_texture() -> PIL -> GPU upload
  This happens ~66 times per frame (once per visible unit).

Replace with:
  1. At startup/first-use: build the unit atlas (unit_atlas.py already does this)
  2. Create ONE Ursina Texture from the atlas, keep it GPU-resident
  3. For each unit billboard: set texture = atlas_texture (once, at entity creation)
  4. Per frame: compute (anim_clip, frame_index) -> UV rect in atlas -> update billboard UV coords
  5. No PIL, no pygame.image.tobytes(), no per-frame texture upload

ADDITIONAL FIXES (same round):
  A. Cache clips_for() result on the entity at spawn time (hero_sprite_library.py)
     - Currently called per-unit per-frame, rebuilds clip dicts
     - Cache on first call, invalidate only on unit type change
  B. Guard ent.position writes:
     - In sync_billboard_entity (or equivalent), skip position update if unchanged
     - Avoids triggering Panda3D transform invalidation for stationary units
  C. Replace time.time() with Ursina's frame time in _unit_anim_state:
     - time.time() has 15ms granularity on Windows
     - Use time.perf_counter() or accumulate dt passed from update()

FILES YOU MAY EDIT:
- game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py
- game/graphics/unit_atlas.py
- game/graphics/terrain_texture_bridge.py (minimize changes, keep fog path working)
- game/graphics/hero_sprite_library.py
- game/graphics/ursina_entity_render_collab.py (if position guard lives here)
- game/graphics/ursina_units_anim.py (if timing lives here)

FILES YOU MUST NOT BREAK:
- Fog overlay (terrain_texture_bridge still used for fog — don't remove, just bypass for units)
- Building rendering (buildings use different texture path)
- The instanced renderer path (leave KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCING codepath intact)

VERIFICATION:
- python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick (must PASS)
- python -m tests.perf_ursina_stress (must show FPS improvement)
- All unit types (hero, enemy, peasant, guard, tax collector) must animate visually

DO NOT COMMIT. DO NOT push or version-bump.
Update your agent log at .cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_03_TechnicalDirector_Architecture.json.

Phase 4 is architecturally correct and worth keeping (16 instanced GeomNodes vs 2083 individual NodePaths is the right shape for the future), but does not close the DoD FPS gate alone.

wave5_top5_hot_spots.name: ensure_fog_overlay early-out broken post-WK53
wave5_top5_hot_spots.root_cause: Early-out gate `if int(fog_revision) == my_rev and self._r._fog_entity is not None: return` was designed for legacy flat-terrain fog quad. WK53 heightmap shader path destroys _fog_entity and uploads fog as shader uniform instead, so `_fog_entity is not None` always evaluates False post-WK53. 62,500-iteration tile loop + GPU upload runs every single frame whether or not fog_revision actually advanced.
wave5_top5_hot_spots.fix: Add `or self._r._terrain_ground_entity is not None` (or equivalent heightmap-aware check) to the early-out condition. Single-line patch.

wave5_top5_hot_spots.name: Dead cave-entrance fragment shader cost
wave5_top5_hot_spots.root_cause: 8 separate distance() calls + min() chain per fragment for cave-entrance hole effect. CPU-side path (update_cave_entrance_shader) was disabled by an early return in WK57, but GPU fragment shader still pays the cost on every fragment. 1920x1080 = 1.6M fragments paying dead cost.
wave5_top5_hot_spots.fix: Wrap dead distance/min block in `if (cave_hole_radius > 0.0)` so GPU dead-code-eliminates.

wave5_top5_hot_spots.name: Unconditional building position/color writes
wave5_top5_hot_spots.root_cause: Per-building ent.position and ent.color are written every frame unconditionally; only scale is dirty-checked. With ~50 buildings, ~1.5-2ms recoverable.
wave5_top5_hot_spots.fix: Dirty-check position and color, mirroring existing _ks_last_scale pattern.

wave5_top5_hot_spots.name: Unit atlas billboard position/color writes + HP bar gating
wave5_top5_hot_spots.location: (unit sync paths in ursina renderer collabs)
wave5_top5_hot_spots.fix: Position dirty check on hero/enemy/peasant/guard atlas billboards + HP-bar gating (don't update HP-bar nodes when HP unchanged).

round_meta.build_target: Fix P0 regression: trees not visible at runtime after Wave 4 (Phase 4 tree instancing) but peasants still chop them. Sim data intact, render-only bug. Agent 03 to diagnose, fix, screenshot-confirm trees visible. NOT a perf wave â€” this is a correctness/visual recovery wave.

pm_status_summary.summary: Wave 7 DONE. Agent 03 diagnosed and fixed WK58-BUG-004 (invisible instanced trees). ROOT CAUSE: two Panda3D-level subtleties. (1) Bounding-volume bug: instanced GeomNodes had MODEL-LOCAL bsphere (e.g. center=(0,0.6,0) r=0.7), but per-instance world positions are computed in the shader from a buffer texture. Panda3D's view-frustum cull saw the local-space bsphere as out-of-view and dropped the ENTIRE instanced draw every frame even though set_instance_count(N)>0 and the buffer texture had valid world positions. (2) Material auto-bind bug: custom GLSL fragment shader read p3d_Material.baseColor, but Panda3D only auto-binds p3d_Material via the auto-shader generator â€” custom shaders see uninitialized struct values (cyan), so trees rendered as cyan teardrops even after bounds fix. FIX: (a) OmniBoundingVolume() + set_final(True) on each per-model NodePath so cull always passes; (b) _bake_per_geom_material_to_vertex_colors() mutates loaded GeomNode at load time, adding vertex-color column populated per-Geom from MaterialAttrib (handles Kenney multi-Geom layouts: trunk+foliage+crown); (c) GLSL shader rewritten to read p3d_Color (baked) and pass to fragment. Env var KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCED_TREES default stays '1'. PM confirmed via screenshot inspection: trees visible at full forest density. Agent 03 added regression test TestInstancedTreeVisibility::test_instanced_geom_uses_omni_bounds_post_setup (loads real Kenney GLB offscreen, asserts active_count>=1 and bounds string contains 'infinite'). Pytest 6/6 PASS. qa_smoke PASS in both env modes. Trees honored no-density-cut directive: all 2,083 trees register.

wave7_perf_post_fix.explanation: Slight FPS regression vs Wave 6 (31.0 -> 28.4) is the cost of correctly rendering trees: OmniBoundingVolume() disables per-GeomNode view-frustum cull on the 16 instanced models (CPU chunk filter still trims per-instance draws to ~341 at default cam, so GPU vertex hit is modest), plus load-time vertex-color bake. Acceptable tradeoff: trees are back at full forest density, baseline is still +14.9 FPS (2.1x improvement).

wave7_screenshots.pm_visual_verdict: Trees visible at full forest density on revealed map. Aesthetic: cyan-tinted foliage + brown trunks (Kenney pack colors after bake). Different palette than pre-WK58. AWAITING JAIMIE PLAYTEST VERDICT for whether the look is acceptable or needs tuning.

round_meta.build_target: Implement Wave 5 candidate 4.C: replace 62,500-vert custom Perlin Mesh with Panda3D GeoMipTerrain (distance-based LOD). Target: close the zoom-out FPS drop Jaimie surfaced post-Wave-7. Behind env flag KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN (default '0' until visually validated). Hard requirement: get_terrain_height() must return IDENTICAL heights with flag on or off (prop/unit/building Y-placement, hero click pick, camera clamp all depend on it). Preserve WK53 fog-of-war shader on terrain. Preserve castle flat area.

pm_status_summary.summary: Wave 8 ran 4.C GeoMipTerrain LOD as a flag-gated dual path. Jaimie playtested with KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN='1' on 2026-05-19 and reported NO measurable zoom-out FPS improvement plus a visible elevation regression (heightfield 250 -> 513 bilinear resample distorts heights at distance). Decision: stay on env='0' (custom Mesh path, the existing WK53 implementation). The GeoMipTerrain code remains in the repo behind the env flag for future research but is NOT the default and should not be re-proposed without a different heightfield strategy. See memory project_geomipterrain_lod_negative_result.md. Sprint perf wins from W2/W3/W4/W6/W7 are unaffected and stand: 13.5 -> 28.4 FPS post-/revealmap (W7 final), +14.9 FPS / 2.1x improvement over the pre-WK58 baseline. Wave 8 outcome: negative result correctly identified and documented; no further action on this path.

wave8_jaimie_verdict.decision: Leave KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN default '0' (custom Mesh). Code stays in repo behind flag, dormant.

wave8_what_we_learned: The zoom-out FPS pain Jaimie originally felt post-W6 may have been less severe than estimated (W7 trees-back + GeoMipTerrain-off is apparently 'a good level'); the actual remaining GPU cost is elsewhere.
Heightfield resampling to fit GeoMipTerrain's 2^n+1 dimension expectation drifts elevation enough to be visually noticeable at distance.
Future GPU-side work should target 4.B (simpler fog fragment shader) rather than 4.C unless the heightfield resolution strategy changes.

pm_agent_prompts.03: You are Agent 03 (TechnicalDirector_Architecture) — diagnostic round for sprint wk59_lair_diagnostic. BUG: Lairs have disappeared from the game since ~WK54 POI updates. All lair code is intact (entities, systems, config, rendering). Your job is to DIAGNOSE the root cause, not fix it yet. DIAGNOSTIC STEPS: (1) Add a temporary print statement in game/systems/lairs.py spawn_initial_lairs() to log how many lairs were created and their grid positions — right after the placement loop. (2) Add a temporary print in game/sim_engine.py setup_initial_state() after the lair_system.spawn_initial_lairs() call to print len(self.lair_system.lairs). (3) Run the game: python main.py --no-llm. Check console output — did 4 lairs get placed? What positions? (4) If lairs placed: use /revealmap cheat command to reveal the full map. Do lairs appear? This tests the fog-of-war hypothesis. (5) Check ursina_renderer.py line ~963 — lairs require Visibility.VISIBLE (real-time LoS), not Visibility.SEEN (explored). On the 250x250 map with castle vision=10 tiles and lair min distance=18 tiles, lairs are ALWAYS in deep fog at game start. (6) Check if enemy spawn slot starvation is happening — print remaining_slots in sim_engine.py before lair spawning (line ~680). (7) Report ALL findings in your agent log. Include: lair count, positions, visibility state, /revealmap results, spawn slot state. DO NOT make permanent code changes. Remove debug prints after testing. Update your agent log at .cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_03_TechnicalDirector_Architecture.json.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Bounty flags invisible in Ursina 3D mode
repro_steps: python main.py --no-llm
Press B to place a bounty flag
Observe: nothing visible at the placed location
pm_bug_tickets.expected: A visible flag with pole, pennant, and $reward text
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Nothing renders. ursina_renderer.py has no bounty sync code. pygame BountyRenderer is bypassed in Ursina mode.
pm_bug_tickets.root_cause: ursina_renderer.py has _sync_snapshot_* methods for heroes/enemies/peasants/guards/projectiles but nothing for bounties. The pygame bounty_renderer.py is skipped when skip_pygame_world=True (always true in Ursina mode).

pm_feature_requests.title: Wave Events System (Option B)
pm_feature_requests.spec: New WaveEventSystem fires named siege events every 2-4 minutes starting at minute 3. Each event: name, enemy composition, spawn direction, 10s HUD warning, wave-clear gold reward. Escalates to boss waves at 20+ minutes. See plan Feature 1 for full wave table.

pm_feature_requests.title: Aggressive Lair Spawn Tuning
pm_feature_requests.spec: Faster lair spawn intervals, larger packs, MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES 32->48, LAIR_INITIAL_COUNT 4->5, min distance 18->15 tiles. See plan Feature 2 for full tuning table.

pm_feature_requests.title: Guild Hero Cap (8 per guild)
pm_feature_requests.spec: HiringBuilding.can_hire() checks heroes_hired < max_heroes (default 8). New GUILD_MAX_HEROES config. try_hire_hero() shows 'Guild is full!' if at cap. Dead heroes free a slot.

pm_feature_requests.title: Double Hero Cost (50->100 gold)
pm_feature_requests.spec: Change HeroConfig.hire_cost from 50 to 100 in config.py. Single line change.

pm_feature_requests.title: Guardhouse Shoots Arrows
pm_feature_requests.spec: Add ranged attack to Guardhouse: 8-tile range, 12 damage, 2s cooldown. Fires ranged_projectile events (reuse existing projectile system from BallistaTower). In addition to existing guard spawning. New config: GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_RANGE_TILES, GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_DAMAGE, GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_COOLDOWN.

pm_feature_requests.title: Starting Buildings (Rangers, Warriors, Market, Guardhouse)
pm_feature_requests.spec: In SimEngine.setup_initial_state(), place 4 buildings pre-constructed around castle: Warriors Guild (128,124), Rangers Guild (119,124), Marketplace (124,128), Guardhouse (124,131). All is_constructed=True, full HP, path tiles, fog revealers. New STARTING_BUILDINGS config list.

pm_feature_requests.title: Bounty Flags Visible in Ursina 3D
pm_feature_requests.spec: Add _sync_snapshot_bounties() to ursina_renderer.py. Create Ursina Entity for each unclaimed bounty: pole (cylinder/line), flag pennant (gold quad), $reward text. Manage _bounty_entities dict keyed by bounty_id. Call from update(). Ensure GameSnapshot includes bounty data.

pm_feature_requests.title: Difficulty Setting (Easy/Normal/Hard) with Lock
pm_feature_requests.spec: New game/systems/difficulty.py with DifficultySystem. Three levels with multiplier tables affecting: spawn intervals, enemies per wave/lair tick, enemy HP on spawn, enemy damage on spawn. Does NOT affect gold costs, hero costs, or economy. Changeable mid-game from pause menu. Lock Difficulty button with confirmation dialog ('Once you lock difficulty, it can't be unlocked for the remaining duration of the game. Are you sure?'). New DifficultyConfig in config.py. Agent 08 builds the menu UI in R2.

pm_feature_requests.title: Dev Mode (Environment Variable)
pm_feature_requests.spec: Activated by $env:KINGDOM_DEV_MODE=1 before launch. Effects: HERO_HIRE_COST=0, STARTING_GOLD=999999, GUILD_MAX_HEROES=999, difficulty defaults to Easy. HUD shows '[DEV MODE]' label. Console prints banner. Pure config overrides at bottom of config.py.

pm_agent_prompts.05:
You are Agent 05 (GameplaySystemsDesigner) activated for sprint wk60_v159_gameplay_fun, round wk60_r1_core_gameplay. GOAL: Make Kingdom Sim actually fun and challenging. You own 7 features. Read the full plan: .cursor/plans/v1.6_gameplay_fun_roadmap.plan.md

FEATURE 1 — WAVE EVENTS SYSTEM (highest priority):
Create game/systems/wave_events.py with a WaveEventSystem(GameSystem). Fire named siege events on a timer: first event at ~3 minutes, then every 2-4 minutes. Each event has: name, enemy_composition (list of type+count), spawn_direction, 10s warning before arrival, wave-clear gold reward. Use the wave table in the plan (Goblin Raid at 3:00, Wolf Pack at 5:30, etc). After the table exhausts, cycle with increasing count multipliers. Wave events can temporarily exceed MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES by up to 50%. Emit EventBus events for HUD warnings ('wave_incoming' with name + seconds) and wave clear ('wave_cleared' with reward). Also tune the existing trickle spawner in game/systems/spawner.py: mixed enemy types (not Goblins-only), raise enemies_per_wave cap from 4 to 6, reduce spawn interval floor from 21000ms to 15000ms, increase interval reduction from -250ms to -500ms.

FEATURE 2 — LAIR SPAWN TUNING:
In game/entities/lair.py, update each lair subclass: faster spawn intervals and larger packs per the plan table. In config.py: MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES 32->48, LAIR_INITIAL_COUNT 4->5, LAIR_MIN_DISTANCE_FROM_CASTLE_TILES 18->15.

FEATURE 3 — GUILD HERO CAP:
In game/entities/buildings/hiring_mixin.py: add max_heroes attribute (default 8 from new GUILD_MAX_HEROES config). can_hire() returns False if heroes_hired >= max_heroes. In game/engine.py try_hire_hero(): check can_hire() before deducting gold; emit an event or set a message for 'Guild is full!' HUD feedback. IMPORTANT: dead heroes must free a slot — ensure heroes_hired is decremented when a hero from that guild dies (check hero death handling in engine.py or hero.py).

FEATURE 4 — HERO COST:
In config.py HeroConfig: change hire_cost from 50 to 100.

FEATURE 5 — GUARDHOUSE ARROWS:
In game/entities/buildings/defensive.py Guardhouse class: add ranged attack. Properties: attack_range_tiles=8.0, arrow_damage=12, arrow_cooldown_sec=2.0. In update(): find nearest enemy within range, fire a ranged_projectile event (look at how BallistaTower does it in the same file for the pattern). Add config values: GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_RANGE_TILES, GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_DAMAGE, GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_COOLDOWN. The guardhouse still spawns guards as before — arrow shooting is IN ADDITION.

FEATURE 8 — DIFFICULTY SYSTEM (Easy/Normal/Hard):
Create game/systems/difficulty.py with DifficultySystem. Three levels with multiplier tables. Multipliers affect ONLY monster-related values at runtime: spawn intervals (lair + trickle), enemies per wave/lair tick, enemy HP on spawn, enemy damage on spawn. Does NOT affect gold costs, hero costs, building prices, bounty costs, or any economy values. Easy: spawn_interval x1.5 (slower), enemy counts x0.6, HP x0.7, damage x0.7. Normal: all x1.0. Hard: spawn_interval x0.7 (faster), enemy counts x1.5, HP x1.3, damage x1.3. Default: Normal. Changeable mid-game (Agent 08 builds the menu UI in R2). Add lock_difficulty() method — once locked, set_difficulty() returns False. Already-spawned enemies are NOT retroactively modified. Add DifficultyConfig dataclass in config.py with all multiplier values as tunable fields. Wire DifficultySystem into spawner.py, lair.py, wave_events.py, and enemy.py — all these systems read multipliers from the shared DifficultySystem instance.

FEATURE 9 — DEV MODE:
Activated by env var KINGDOM_DEV_MODE=1 (checked once at startup). In config.py: add DEV_MODE = os.getenv('KINGDOM_DEV_MODE', '0') == '1'. When DEV_MODE is True, override: HERO_HIRE_COST=0, STARTING_GOLD=999999, GUILD_MAX_HEROES=999. Print '*** DEV MODE ACTIVE — free heroes, infinite gold ***' to console. Agent 08 will add the '[DEV MODE]' HUD label in R2. Keep it simple — just override the config values, no parallel config path.

AFTER ALL CHANGES: Run python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick and python -m pytest tests/ -x -q. Update your agent log. DO NOT COMMIT.
pm_agent_prompts.03:
You are Agent 03 (TechnicalDirector_Architecture) activated for sprint wk60_v159_gameplay_fun, round wk60_r1_core_gameplay. GOAL: Two features. Read the full plan: .cursor/plans/v1.6_gameplay_fun_roadmap.plan.md

FEATURE 6 — STARTING BUILDINGS:
In game/sim_engine.py setup_initial_state(): After the castle is placed at (124,124) 3x3, add 4 pre-constructed buildings: Warriors Guild at grid (128,124), Rangers Guild at (119,124), Marketplace at (124,128), Guardhouse at (124,131). All 2x2. Follow the exact same pattern used for castle placement: create the building entity, set is_constructed=True, full HP, place path tiles under footprint, register fog-of-war revealers. Add a STARTING_BUILDINGS config list in config.py for easy tuning. Verify no grid overlaps with castle footprint (124-126, 124-126).

FEATURE 7 — BOUNTY FLAGS IN URSINA 3D:
In game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py: Add _sync_snapshot_bounties() method. For each unclaimed bounty in the game snapshot: create an Ursina Entity with a vertical pole (brown cylinder or scaled cube) and a triangular flag pennant (gold-colored quad, billboard mode). Add a Text entity showing '$reward' above the flag. Cache entities in _bounty_entities: dict[int, Entity] keyed by bounty_id. On each frame, create new entities for new bounties, remove entities for claimed/expired bounties. Call _sync_snapshot_bounties() from the update() method alongside the other _sync_snapshot_* calls. IMPORTANT: First check if GameSnapshot already includes bounty data (positions, rewards, IDs, claimed status). If not, add it to the snapshot.

AFTER ALL CHANGES: Run python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick and python -m pytest tests/ -x -q. Update your agent log. DO NOT COMMIT.
